title_en: Al-Mahalli's Original Work: The Makkan Surahs and Al-Fatiha
title_ar: تفسير الجلالين — من سورة الأنفال إلى هود
content_en:
Al-Mahalli's portion of the tafsir covers Surahs al-Kahf through al-Nas (chapters 18–114), encompassing the shorter Makkan surahs that form the core of the Quran recited in daily prayer, as well as the longer Makkan surahs of the middle Quran. He also wrote the commentary on Surah al-Fatiha, which stands at the opening of the Quran and serves as the gateway to the text as a whole.

The Makkan surahs are characterized by their focus on theological fundamentals: the oneness of God, the reality of the resurrection and judgment, the stories of the prophets and the communities that rejected them, and the nature of the Quran itself. Al-Mahalli's grammatical and lexical method is ideally suited to these surahs, which employ the full range of Arabic rhetorical power — ellipsis, emphasis, contrasting parallel structures, dramatic address — to convey theological truth with aesthetic force.

Surah al-Kahf, which begins al-Mahalli's section, contains four famous narratives: the People of the Cave, the two garden owners, Musa and Khidr, and Dhul-Qarnayn. Each narrative carries theological weight — the first addresses the test of faith and community, the second the test of wealth and ingratitude, the third the limits of human knowledge before divine wisdom, and the fourth the question of righteous kingship and the protection of civilization. Al-Mahalli's commentary clarifies the grammatical structure of each narrative, supplies the elliptical elements, and provides basic contextual glosses without turning the exegesis into a philosophical essay.

The shorter surahs of the final juz — the thirtieth part of the Quran — receive particularly attentive treatment. These surahs, which most Muslims memorize first, are also among the most grammatically dense and rhetorically concentrated in the entire text. The commentary on Surah al-Zalzalah, al-Adiyat, al-Qari'ah, and the other eschatological surahs clarifies the imagery of the Day of Judgment — the shaking of the earth, the weighing of deeds, the scattered people — in straightforward, accessible terms.

Al-Fatiha, which al-Mahalli wrote last (though it appears first in the Quran), receives a commentary that is proportionally longer than most of the short surahs — reflecting its theological density. The seven verses of al-Fatiha contain an affirmation of God's lordship, mercy, and sovereignty over the Day of Judgment, followed by the declaration of worship and petition for guidance, then the request to be placed on the path of those whom God has blessed rather than those who have earned anger or gone astray. Al-Mahalli's commentary identifies the grammatical subject of 'alladhina an'amta alayhim' (those upon whom You have bestowed favor), clarifies the distinction between 'al-maghdub alayhim' and 'al-dallin' as understood in the prophetic tradition, and establishes the verse's context within the surah's overall prayer structure.
title_id: Karya Asli al-Mahalli: Surah-surah Makkiyah dan al-Fatihah
content_id:
Bagian al-Mahalli dari tafsir mencakup Surah al-Kahf hingga al-Nas (surah ke-18 hingga ke-114), yang meliputi surah-surah Makkiyah yang lebih pendek yang membentuk inti Al-Qur'an yang dibaca dalam shalat harian, serta surah-surah Makkiyah yang lebih panjang dari bagian tengah Al-Qur'an. Beliau juga menulis komentar untuk Surah al-Fatihah, yang berdiri di awal Al-Qur'an dan berfungsi sebagai gerbang menuju teks secara keseluruhan.

Surah-surah Makkiyah dicirikan oleh fokusnya pada dasar-dasar teologis: keesaan Allah, realitas kebangkitan dan hari pembalasan, kisah-kisah para nabi dan kaum-kaum yang menolak mereka, serta seruan kepada pengabdian yang tulus dan bertauhid. Al-Mahalli mendekati surah-surah ini dengan kepakaran linguistiknya yang khas, mengidentifikasi nuansa kosakata dan konstruksi gramatikal yang membawa implikasi teologis dan etis dari masing-masing ayat.

Perlakuan al-Mahalli terhadap surah-surah seperti al-Kahf, Maryam, Tha-Ha, dan al-Anbiya — yang mengandung kisah-kisah nabi yang panjang — mencontohkan kemampuannya untuk menyajikan materi naratif secara ringkas tanpa kehilangan poin-poin esensial. Kisah-kisah para nabi dalam Al-Qur'an bukan sekadar narasi — masing-masing membawa pesan teologis dan moral yang spesifik, dan identifikasi singkat al-Mahalli tentang poin-poin tersebut membantu pelajar memahami apa yang dimaksudkan setiap surah untuk diajarkan.

Surah-surah yang lebih pendek dalam Juz' 'Amma (Juz ke-30) mendapat perhatian khusus dari al-Mahalli karena surah-surah ini yang paling sering dihafalkan oleh para pelajar dan paling sering dibaca dalam shalat. Penjelasannya yang ringkas tentang Surah al-Ikhlas, al-Falaq, an-Nas, dan yang lainnya telah menjadi hafalan standar bagi jutaan Muslim — tidak hanya teks Al-Qur'an itu sendiri tetapi penjelasan Jalalain tentangnya.

Komentar al-Mahalli tentang al-Fatihah mendapat posisi istimewa dalam keseluruhan karya karena al-Fatihah adalah surah yang dibaca dalam setiap rakaat shalat oleh setiap Muslim yang shalat. Analisisnya tentang setiap kata dan frase dalam surah ini — nama-nama dan sifat-sifat Allah yang disebutkan di dalamnya, makna "al-hamd" (segala puji), signifikansi "al-Rahman al-Rahim" (Yang Maha Pengasih lagi Maha Penyayang), dan doa petunjuk dalam "ihdina al-shirath al-mustaqim" (tunjukilah kami jalan yang lurus) — telah membantu generasi-generasi pelajar memahami surah yang mereka baca puluhan kali sehari.

Warisan bagian al-Mahalli dari Jalalain terletak pada kemampuannya untuk membuat surah-surah teologis murni dari Al-Qur'an Makkiyah mudah diakses oleh pelajar. Ketika seorang pelajar membaca tafsirnya tentang Surah Ya-Sin atau Surah al-Rahman untuk pertama kalinya, mereka mendapatkan kunci linguistik yang mereka butuhkan untuk memahami teks tanpa harus menyaring volume besar komentar. Disiplin metodologis al-Mahalli — mengatakan yang cukup dan tidak lebih — adalah pencapaian keilmuan yang luar biasa yang terus melayani pelajar lintas generasi dan benua.
content_ar: Al-Mahalli's portion of the التفسير covers السور al-Kahf through al-Nas (الأبواب 18–114), encompassing the shorter Makkan السور that form the الأساسي of the القرآن الكريم recited in daily الصلاة, as well as the longer Makkan السور of the middle القرآن الكريم. He أيضاً wrote the commentary on السورة al-Fatiha, which stands at the opening of the القرآن الكريم and serves as the gateway to the text as a whole. The Makkan السور are characterized by their focus on theological fundamentals: the oneness of God, the reality of the البعث and judgment, the stories of the الأنبياء and the communities that rejected them, and the nature of the القرآن الكريم itself. Al-Mahalli's grammatical and lexical method is ideally suited to these السور, which employ the full range of Arabic rhetorical power — ellipsis, emphasis, contrasting parallel structures, dramatic address — to convey theological الحق with aesthetic force. السورة al-Kahf, which begins al-Mahalli's الفصل, contains four famous narratives: the People of the Cave, the two garden owners, موسى ﷺ and Khidr, and Dhul-Qarnayn. Each narrative carries theological weight — the أولاً addresses the الاختبار of الإيمان and الجماعة, the ثانياً the الاختبار of الثروة and ingratitude, the ثالثاً the limits of human العلم قبل divine الحكمة, and the رابعاً the question of الصالح kingship and the protection of civilization. Al-Mahalli's commentary clarifies the grammatical structure of each narrative, supplies the elliptical elements, and provides الأساسي contextual glosses without turning the exegesis into a philosophical essay. The shorter السور of the final juz — the thirtieth part of the القرآن الكريم — receive particularly attentive treatment. These السور, which most المسلمون memorize أولاً, are أيضاً among the most grammatically dense and rhetorically concentrated in the entire text. The commentary on السورة al-Zalzalah, al-Adiyat, al-Qari'ah, and the other eschatological السور clarifies the imagery of the يوم القيامة — the shaking of the earth, the weighing of deeds, the scattered people — in straightforward, accessible terms. Al-Fatiha, which al-Mahalli wrote last (though it appears أولاً in the القرآن الكريم), receives a commentary that is proportionally longer than most of the short السور — reflecting its theological density. The seven الآيات of al-Fatiha contain an affirmation of God's lordship, الرحمة, and sovereignty over the يوم القيامة, followed by the declaration of worship and petition for الهداية, then the request to be placed on the الطريق of those whom God has blessed rather than those who have earned الغضب or gone astray. Al-Mahalli's commentary identifies the grammatical subject of 'alladhina an'amta alayhim' (those upon whom You have bestowed النعمة), clarifies the distinction between 'al-maghdub alayhim' and 'al-dallin' as understood in the prophetic التراث, and establishes the الآية's context within the السورة's overall الصلاة structure.
